architecture_fit.note: Minimal softmax->sigmoid-gate delta solves the task at 100%; key insight that softmax structurally can't trace L>1 paths is genuine, though the path is a single-step lookup of a memorized pathmask rather than stepwise tree navigation.

baseline_comparison.note: Strong strawman: softmax on identical logits hits its provable 7/128=5.5% ceiling, plus query-knockout and uniform baselines under matched conditions.

faithfulness.note: Query-knockout ablation (zeroing the query readout) collapses accuracy to 0%, showing the circuit causally depends on the query input, not a stored label; output is driven only by tokens (model_fn never sees the path).

operating_range.note: Gaussian-noise sweep sigma 0..2 x path length shows graceful, depth-dependent degradation (deeper paths break first, the (1-p)^L signature) with the breakpoint made explicit.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set circuit: the fixed Knuth-DP tree is written into the embedding table by hand as an associative memory; no training.

visual_judgement.note: Three legible panels (interactive query->path bars, sigmoid-vs-softmax accuracy with the ceiling marked, noise x depth heatmap); minor caveat that the path panel reconstructs attention from the stored path rather than the live tensor.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties each panel to the smallest artefact that would flip the claim, with the right y-axis (perfect-path-trace fraction), the right strawman, and the right grain.

notes: A sharp, well-argued hand-built attempt whose central contribution is the structural insight that a sum-to-1 softmax head is mathematically capped at 7/128 on this multi-node-path metric while an independent sigmoid gate clears it at 100%, backed by a matched-logit strawman and a causal query-knockout ablation. The hardcoded tree-in-embedding circuit, noise operating-range sweep, and clear three-panel visualisation all land. Main limitation: the mechanism stores and reads out the whole optimal path as a precomputed mask in one step rather than navigating the tree sequentially, so it demonstrates attention can *attend to* the path more than *search* it.
